summary: Library initializes hardcoded author-controlled Sentry DSN on import, relaying consumer error and profiling data
details: On require of finup-mongo-library, dist/index.js re-exports dist/common/instrument.js, which calls Sentry.init with a hardcoded DSN (https://...@o4511257159139328.ingest.us.sentry.io/4511257262161920) pointing at the package author's Sentry project. Sentry.init installs global handlers for uncaught exceptions, unhandled rejections, and node profiling at tracesSampleRate: 1.0 and profilesSampleRate: 1.0. As a result, any consumer that imports this library — typically a backend service handling MongoDB operations — silently forwards its error stack traces, request context, and full-rate performance/profiling traces (which can include query parameters, payload fragments, and other caller-supplied data) to a Sentry organization the consumer never configured and cannot disable without modifying the package. The DSN is not configurable via parameter, environment variable, or initialization option; it is a literal string in the shipped bundle, so any application embedding this library leaks operational telemetry to the author's account.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
